title: sideroxylon saxorum var. collinum
intro: Sideroxylon saxorum var. collinum, also known as the smooth-barked or Collin's bull oak, is a plant species within the family Sapotaceae, native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits associated with Sideroxylon saxorum var. collinum include wound healing, reduced fever, and relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used in traditional medicine practices in Australia and other parts of the world.
